overall_summary: Big gap to reference, but the data shows exactly where it is hiding. Attack these points. Current gap: 15.266s slower than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 1 (+11.854s), where 89.0 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. Executing these fixes could recover 2.30s — a completely different lap time.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 44.2 km/h below reference (50.2 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 94 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 50.2 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-44.2 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Entry speed is 97.1 km/h below reference but apex speed is acceptable — over-braking on entry.
priority_actions.instruction: Trail the brakes into the corner rather than releasing fully at turn-in. Carry more entry speed and use trail braking to rotate the car to the apex.

priority_actions.issue: Braking 10m too early compared to reference.
priority_actions.instruction: Move the brake marker 10m later. Reference brakes at 1335m, driver at 1325m.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 89.0 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
Average throttle application is 6% lower than reference — driver is hesitant getting back on power.
Solid sector — 1.617s ahead of reference.
sector_feedback.details: Corner speed is 15.6 km/h above reference — good commitment through the bends.

all_issues.fix: Bring the brake point back by 10m for consistency. Reference uses 325m.
